items.title: Cancellation, timeout and stale results: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications misconception? Dropping a promise variable automatically cancels its underlying request.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Promises do not cancel by being ignored; pass and trigger an AbortSignal to supported operations.

items.title: Cancellation, timeout and stale results: verified implementation facet
items.prompt: Abort obsolete work and prevent older responses from overwriting current interface state. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating AbortController prevents obsolete work; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted authoring draft. Runtime evidence does not replace independent human standards, pedagogy, accessibility, answer-oracle, and formal-exam review.

hints: Implement the smallest artifact that satisfies the declared observable contract, then check the visible case before considering the hidden boundary.

feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic authoring check failed; inspect the exact artifact, interaction state, viewport, and observable result without assuming hidden-case details.

criteria.description: Passes the visible and hidden deterministic checks for the declared implementation facet in its pinned authoring environment.

privateAuthorNotes: This code item and its oracle are AI-assisted draft material with reviewer=null and examEligibility=false.
Browser-verifier items are authoring evidence only and must never be routed to the official untrusted-code runner or formal exam pipeline.
items.starterCode: // TODO: implement the declared bounded browser behavior.

answer.referenceSolution: const controller = new AbortController(); globalThis.cancelRequest = () => controller.abort(); async function load() { const output = document.querySelector("output"); output.textContent = "loading"; try { const response = await fetch("https://learncoding.test/api/lesson", { signal: controller.signal }); if (!response.ok) throw new Error("HTTP " + response.status); const body = await response.json(); output.textContent = body.title; } catch (error) { output.textContent = error.name === "AbortError" ? "cancelled" : "failed"; } } load();
answer.explanation: The reference is a bounded javascript artifact for AbortController prevents obsolete work. Passing proves only the declared observable facet, not holistic mastery or human accessibility approval.
